meta.tagline: The PNW startup pitch competition directory

competitions.name: Demolicious
competitions.description: Portland's monthly pitch event reintroduced in 2024 by UpStart Collective. Founders get 3 minutes to pitch, audience votes for the winner. Monthly champion advances to an annual Championship Showdown.
competitions.notes: Actively accepting pitch applications for 2026. Sign up at their website.

competitions.name: Westside Pitch / Columbia River Pitch / Collegiate Startup Challenge
competitions.description: TiE Oregon runs multiple pitch competitions across Oregon and SW Washington. Competitions offer equity investment, cash prizes, and professional services to tech and traded-sector startups.
competitions.notes: Westside Pitch confirmed April 23, 2026 in Hillsboro. Applications closed March 9 — monitor for next cycle.

competitions.name: Pitch Please / Angel Oregon / Pop Up & Pitch
competitions.description: OEN runs year-round pitch programming including the monthly Pitch Please, Angel Oregon investment programs for CPG and tech founders, and Pop Up & Pitch food/bev events.
competitions.notes: Angel Oregon CPG 2026 program active now — Pitch Day in April. Pitch Please runs monthly.

competitions.name: PSU Pitch Events / Invent Oregon Partner
competitions.description: Portland State University's Center for Entrepreneurship is a founding partner of Invent Oregon and hosts its own pitch and entrepreneurial programming throughout the academic year.

competitions.name: Invent Oregon (InventOR) Collegiate Challenge
competitions.description: Oregon's only statewide collegiate prototyping competition. Student teams receive $2,000 development grants and compete for $30,000 in prizes at the annual June finals.
competitions.notes: 2026 finals scheduled for June. Bootcamp in May. Apply through your school.

competitions.name: OBI / OTRADI Pitch Events
competitions.description: Oregon's life sciences incubator occasionally hosts pitch and demo events for biotech and health-focused startups. Also a past venue for Founders Live PDX.

competitions.name: VertueLab / 45Camp Accelerator
competitions.description: VertueLab supports early-stage cleantech startups through two accelerators — Cascadia CleanTech Accelerator and 45Camp — plus a Climate Impact Fund for direct investment.
competitions.notes: Accelerator program runs May–September. Watch for application opening in spring.

competitions.name: Cascadia CleanTech Accelerator
competitions.description: 18-week virtual accelerator for pre-seed cleantech startups in the Northwest. Includes mentorship, curriculum, and pitch-based funding competition. Program runs May–September annually.
competitions.notes: Applications typically open in spring for May start. Monitor their site.

competitions.name: Portland Startup Week
competitions.description: Week-long celebration of Portland's startup community including pitch events, workshops, and networking. Includes the annual Demolicious Startup Week edition.
competitions.notes: Portland Startup Week 2026 confirmed May 11–16. Event submissions open.

competitions.name: Founders Live Portland
competitions.description: Monthly pitch competition where founders pitch live to an audience who votes for the winner. New 'Founders Live Build' format for 2026 — 5 founders build apps live, then pitch and demo.
competitions.notes: Next event: April 9, 2026 at Metro Region Innovation Hub, 2130 SW 5th Ave Portland. 6–9pm.

competitions.name: Gorge PitchFest
competitions.description: Annual pitch competition hosted by the Mid-Columbia Innovation Hub serving entrepreneurs in the Columbia River Gorge region on both the Oregon and Washington sides.
competitions.notes: 2026 event: May 28 at The Granada Theatre. Applications open now — apply at their website.

competitions.name: Starve Ups
competitions.description: Portland-based startup pitch and networking event. Monitor their site for upcoming events.

competitions.name: PIE Pitch Events
competitions.description: One of Portland's most established startup incubators, co-founded by Wieden+Kennedy. Has hosted demo days and pitch events for cohorts since 2009.
competitions.notes: All PIE programs listed as 'on hiatus for 2026' on their website. Worth monitoring for a return.

aggregators.name: Calagator
aggregators.description: Portland's community tech calendar. A great first stop for spotting new pitch and startup events.

aggregators.name: Silicon Florist
aggregators.description: Portland's best local startup news source — excellent coverage of pitch events, demo days, and ecosystem news.

aggregators.name: Technology Association of Oregon (TAO)
aggregators.description: Oregon's leading tech industry association. Check their calendar for pitch and innovation events.
